verses.text: The LORD: when any man has a running issue from his flesh, he is unclean. Whatever he lies on or sits on — unclean. Whoever touches what he touched — wash, bathe, unclean till evening. Anyone he touches unwashed — unclean. Earthen vessel — broken; wooden — rinsed. Guidance: A discharge from the body marks days of impurity. The point isn't shame; it's that the camp must guard against any fluid carrying illness. God's laws build slowly the kind of public hygiene the ancient world rarely had.

When cleansed of his issue, number seven days, wash clothes, bathe in running water — clean. Eighth day take two doves or young pigeons to the priest at the tabernacle door — one sin, one burnt offering. Priest atones before the LORD. Guidance: Healing alone isn't the end of the rite. Seven days of waiting, then sacrifice. Even something physical becomes a worship moment. The body's return to wholeness is offered back to God.

Man's seed of copulation — wash flesh, unclean till evening. Garment or skin where the seed touches — washed, unclean till evening. Woman with whom a man has lain — both bathe, unclean till evening. Guidance: Intimacy itself is not sin — only ritually impure until evening. God's law treats the body's life-giving fluids as holy enough to require washing, not so common that they pass without notice.

When a woman has her issue, her flowers — she is in separation seven days. Touched — unclean till evening. Bed or seat — unclean. Whoever touches them — wash, bathe, unclean till evening. If a man lies with her — unclean seven days; every bed he lies on unclean. Guidance: Monthly bleeding sets a woman apart for one week. Not punishment — a rhythm of withdrawal, a body's own pause for renewal. God's law builds in rest that menstruation itself was already asking for.

If a woman has an issue of blood many days out of her separation — unclean as in her separation. Bed, seat — unclean. Touched — wash, bathe, unclean till evening. Cleansed, number seven days. Eighth day take two doves — one sin, one burnt. Priest atones. Guidance: A prolonged bleeding makes her unclean for months. The woman who touched Jesus' hem in Mark 5 lived in this verse for twelve years. He didn't just heal her body — He returned her to the camp.

Thus shall ye separate Israel from their uncleanness, lest they defile My tabernacle that is among them. This is the law of issue, seed, the woman in her separation, of any with issue, and of him that lies with her unclean. Guidance: God dwells among His people. That's why these laws exist. Holiness is not abstract — it's the difference between a tent with God in it and a tent with God absent.
